1	# Copyright (c) OpenMMLab. All rights reserved.
2	"""Qwen3.5 source models for the TurboMind pipeline.
3	
4	This module hosts the full Qwen3.5 family in one place using composition
5	instead of inheritance (mirrors ``InternVLModel``):
6	
7	* ``Qwen3_5TextModel`` -- text-only weight model (dense + linear-attn +
8	  optional MoE). Not registered on its own; used directly by the aggregate.
9	* ``Qwen3_5VisionModel`` -- the vision sub-tree rooted at
10	  ``ModelRoot.vision_model``.
11	* ``Qwen3_5Model`` -- a thin aggregate holding a ``text_model`` and an
12	  optional ``vision_model``, registered as ``qwen3_5`` / ``qwen3_5-moe``. It
13	  delegates the two-phase ``__init__`` / ``bind_runtime`` / ``model(pfx)``
14	  lifecycle to its children, and skips the vision encoder when
15	  ``language_model_only`` is set.
16	
17	The patcher and position embedding are replicated across TP ranks. Vision
18	transformer blocks and merger linears shard with the model TP group.
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import math
23	import re
24	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
25	
26	import _turbomind as _tm
27	import torch
28	
29	from lmdeploy.vl.constants import Modality
30	
31	from ..builders import (
32	    AttentionBuilder,
33	    Builder,
34	    DecoderLayerBuilder,
35	    DecoderLayerConfig,
36	    DeltaNetBuilder,
37	    FfnBuilder,
38	    LayerNormBuilder,
39	    ModuleListBuilder,
40	    ModuleListConfig,
41	    MoeBuilder,
42	    SplitSide,
43	    TextModelBuilder,
44	    VisionModelBuilder,
45	    _act_type_id,
46	    make_layer_norm_config,
47	)
48	from ..builders.attention import split_output_gate
49	from ..linear import Linear, transform_input_dim, transform_output_dim
50	from ..text_model import TextModel
51	from ..weight_format import TrivialFormat
52	from .base import INPUT_MODELS
53	from .utils import (
54	    make_attention_config,
55	    make_ffn_config,
56	    make_model_weight_config,
57	    make_moe_config,
58	    read_packed_moe_expert,
59	    reorder_rotary_emb,
60	)
61	
62	if TYPE_CHECKING:
63	    from transformers.models.qwen3_5.configuration_qwen3_5 import Qwen3_5Config, Qwen3_5TextConfig
64	    from transformers.models.qwen3_5_moe.configuration_qwen3_5_moe import Qwen3_5MoeConfig, Qwen3_5MoeTextConfig
65	
66	
67	def map_packed_qwen35_experts(name: str) -> str:
68	    """Map packed expert names to weight names so parameter.py can classify."""
69	    return re.sub(r'(mlp\.experts\.(?:gate_up|down)_proj)$', r'\1.weight', name)
70	
71	
72	def _center_norm(w):
73	    return 1.0 + w.float()
74	
75	
76	class Qwen3_5TextModel(TextModel):
77	    """Weight model for Qwen3.5 (dense + linear-attn + optional MoE)."""
78	
79	    _loader_mappings = [map_packed_qwen35_experts]
80	    cfg: Qwen3_5TextConfig | Qwen3_5MoeTextConfig
81	
82	    def __init__(self, cfg: Qwen3_5TextConfig | Qwen3_5MoeTextConfig, *, resolver):
83	        super().__init__(cfg, resolver=resolver)
84	
85	        self._attn_cfg = make_attention_config(cfg)
86	        self._attn_cfg.output_gate = True
87	        self._attn_cfg.rope.mrope_interleaved = True
88	
89	        self._n_experts = getattr(cfg, 'num_experts', 0)
90	
91	        # ---- DeltaNet template ----
92	        ln_key_heads = cfg.linear_num_key_heads
93	        ln_val_heads = cfg.linear_num_value_heads
94	        ln_key_dim   = cfg.linear_key_head_dim
95	        ln_val_dim   = cfg.linear_value_head_dim
96	
97	        self._dn_cfg = _tm.DeltaNetConfig()
98	        self._dn_cfg.hidden_dim      = self.cfg.hidden_size
99	        self._dn_cfg.num_k_heads     = ln_key_heads
100	        self._dn_cfg.num_v_heads     = ln_val_heads
101	        self._dn_cfg.key_head_dim    = ln_key_dim
102	        self._dn_cfg.value_head_dim  = ln_val_dim
103	        self._dn_cfg.d_conv          = cfg.linear_conv_kernel_dim or 4
104	
105	        # ---- MoE template ----
106	        if self._n_experts > 0:
107	            self._moe_cfg = make_moe_config(
108	                cfg,
109	                experts_per_token=cfg.num_experts_per_tok)
110	            self._moe_cfg.expert_num = self._n_experts
111	            inter_size=cfg.moe_intermediate_size
112	        else:
113	            inter_size=cfg.intermediate_size
114	
115	        # ---- FFN template ----
116	        self._ffn_cfg = make_ffn_config(
117	            cfg,
118	            act_type=_act_type_id('silu'), inter_size=inter_size)
119	
120	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
121	    # model() — same topology as old code
122	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
123	
124	    def model(self, pfx):
125	        root_cfg = make_model_weight_config(self.cfg)
126	        builder = TextModelBuilder(
127	            root_cfg, self._ctx,
128	            root_handles=self._root_handles,
129	            tp=self._model_tp,
130	            vocab_size=self.cfg.vocab_size)
131	        builder.add_token_embeds(pfx.get('model.language_model.embed_tokens.weight'))
132	        builder.norm = self.norm(pfx + 'model.language_model.norm', _center_norm)
133	        lm_pfx = (pfx + 'model.language_model.embed_tokens'
134	                  if self.cfg.tie_word_embeddings
135	                  else pfx + 'lm_head')
136	        builder.add_lm_head(self._linear(lm_pfx))
137	        builder.layers = self.layers(pfx + 'model.language_model.layers')
138	        builder.build()
139	
140	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
141	    # Attention / linear-attention factories
142	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
143	
144	    def attn(self, pfx):
145	        q, k, v, o = [self._linear(pfx + f'{x}_proj') for x in 'qkvo']
146	
147	        cfg = self._attn_cfg.clone()
148	        q, gate = split_output_gate(q, head_num=cfg.head_num)
149	
150	        def reorder(x):
151	            return reorder_rotary_emb(x, cfg.head_dim, cfg.rope.dim, resolver=self._resolver)
152	
153	        q, k = [reorder(x) for x in (q, k)]
154	
155	        m = AttentionBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._attn_tp)
156	
157	        m.add_qkv_proj(q, k, v, gate=gate)
158	        m.add_o_proj(o)
159	
160	        m.q_norm = self.norm(pfx + 'q_norm',
161	                             lambda w: reorder(_center_norm(w)))
162	        m.k_norm = self.norm(pfx + 'k_norm',
163	                             lambda w: reorder(_center_norm(w)))
164	
165	        return m.build()
166	
167	    def linear_attn(self, pfx):
168	        cfg = self._dn_cfg.clone()
169	        builder = DeltaNetBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._attn_tp)
170	
171	        builder.add_input_projections(
172	            in_proj_qkv=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_qkv'),
173	            in_proj_z=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_z'),
174	            in_proj_b=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_b'),
175	            in_proj_a=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_a'),
176	            out_proj=self._linear(pfx + 'out_proj'))
177	        builder.add_scalar_params(
178	            a_log=pfx.pop('A_log'),
179	            dt_bias=pfx.pop('dt_bias'))
180	        builder.add_conv1d(
181	            pfx.pop('conv1d.weight'))
182	        builder.norm = self.norm(pfx + 'norm')  # not zero-centered
183	        return builder.build()
184	
185	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
186	    # FFN / MoE factories
187	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
188	
189	    def ffn(self, pfx, inter_size, is_expert=False):
190	        try:
191	            w1, w3, w2 = [self._linear(pfx + f'{x}_proj')
192	                          for x in ('gate', 'up', 'down')]
193	        except KeyError:
194	            return None
195	
196	        cfg = self._ffn_cfg.clone()
197	        cfg.inter_size = inter_size
198	        cfg.is_expert  = is_expert
199	
200	        m = FfnBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._mlp_tp)
201	        m.add_ffn(w1, w2, w3)
202	        return m.build()
203	
204	    def moe(self, pfx):
205	        cfg = self._moe_cfg.clone()
206	
207	        m = MoeBuilder(cfg, self._ctx)
208	
209	        m.add_gate('gate', self._linear(pfx + 'gate'))
210	
211	        experts_pfx = pfx + 'experts'
212	        experts = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)
213	        for e in range(self._n_experts):
214	            experts[e] = self._moe_expert_ffn(
215	                experts_pfx, e, self.cfg.moe_intermediate_size)
216	        m.experts = experts.build()
217	
218	        m.add_gate('shared_gate', self._linear(pfx + 'shared_expert_gate'))
219	        shared = self.ffn(pfx + 'shared_expert', self.cfg.shared_expert_intermediate_size)
220	
221	        return m.build(), shared
222	
223	    def _packed_moe_ffn(self, experts_pfx, expert_idx, inter_size):
224	        w1, w2, w3 = read_packed_moe_expert(
225	            experts_pfx + 'gate_up_proj',
226	            experts_pfx + 'down_proj',
227	            expert_idx,
228	            resolver=self._resolver,
229	        )
230	        cfg = self._ffn_cfg.clone()
231	        cfg.inter_size = inter_size
232	        cfg.is_expert  = True
233	        m = FfnBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._mlp_tp)
234	        m.add_ffn(w1, w2, w3)
235	        return m.build()
236	
237	    def _moe_expert_ffn(self, experts_pfx, expert_idx, inter_size):
238	        expert_pfx = experts_pfx + expert_idx
239	        return (self.ffn(expert_pfx, inter_size, is_expert=True)
240	                or self._packed_moe_ffn(experts_pfx, expert_idx, inter_size))
241	
242	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
243	    # layers() — dispatch by layer type
244	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
245	
246	    def layers(self, pfx):
247	        layers = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)
248	        for i, p in pfx.slices(0, self.cfg.num_hidden_layers):
249	            d = DecoderLayerBuilder(DecoderLayerConfig(), self._ctx)
250	            if self.cfg.layer_types[i] == 'linear_attention':
251	                d.linear_attn = self.linear_attn(p + 'linear_attn')
252	            else:
253	                d.attention = self.attn(p + 'self_attn')
254	            if self._n_experts > 0:
255	                d.moe_ffn, d.feed_forward = self.moe(p + 'mlp')
256	            else:
257	                d.feed_forward = self.ffn(p + 'mlp', self.cfg.intermediate_size)
258	            d.attention_norm = self.norm(p + 'input_layernorm', _center_norm)
259	            d.ffn_norm = self.norm(p + 'post_attention_layernorm', _center_norm)
260	            layers[i] = d.build()
261	        return layers.build()
262	
263	
264	# Explicit map: ViT head_dim -> kernel-supported head_dim. The attention
265	# kernel only ships {64,128,192,256,576} and impl_16816.h requires
266	# head_dim % 16 == 0, so head_dim=72 (Qwen3.5-27B ViT) is zero-padded
267	# per-head to 128. Other dims must be added here deliberately — we don't
268	# auto-round-up because the next-supported jump can be large.
269	_VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED = {
270	    64:  64,    # <=2B: native
271	    72:  128,   # >=9B: pad to nearest kernel-supported dim
272	}
273	
274	
275	def _padded_vit_head_dim(real_hd: int) -> int:
276	    if real_hd not in _VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED:
277	        raise NotImplementedError(
278	            f'Qwen3.5 ViT head_dim={real_hd} not supported; '
279	            f'known: {sorted(_VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED)}')
280	    return _VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED[real_hd]
281	
282	
283	def _assert_trivial(linear: Linear, name: str) -> None:
284	    """Padding only handles trivial weights for now.
285	
286	    Callers must gate this on `padded_hd != real_hd` so quantized 2B-style paths stay untouched.
287	    """
288	    if not isinstance(linear.weight_format, TrivialFormat):
289	        raise NotImplementedError(
290	            f'ViT {name} weight is {type(linear.weight_format).__name__}; '
291	            f'head_dim padding currently supports TrivialFormat only '
292	            f'(dequant ViT before padding).')
293	
294	
295	@transform_output_dim
296	def _pad_head_dim_out(t: torch.Tensor, *, num_heads: int, src_hd: int,
297	                      dst_hd: int) -> torch.Tensor:
298	    """Pad each head's OUTPUT block from src_hd to dst_hd; num_heads unchanged.
299	
300	    Applied to Q/K/V projections: weight (in_dim, num_heads*src_hd) becomes (in_dim, num_heads*dst_hd) with the new
301	    [src_hd, dst_hd) slice zeroed. The @transform_output_dim decorator handles bias by promoting it to 2-D and squeezing
302	    back, so the bias is padded the same way as weight.
303	    """
304	    rest = t.shape[:-1]
305	    t = t.reshape(rest + (num_heads, src_hd))
306	    pad = t.new_zeros(rest + (num_heads, dst_hd - src_hd))
307	    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=-1).reshape(rest + (num_heads * dst_hd,))
308	
309	
310	@transform_input_dim
311	def _pad_head_dim_in(t: torch.Tensor, *, num_heads: int, src_hd: int,
312	                     dst_hd: int) -> torch.Tensor:
313	    """Pad each head's INPUT block from src_hd to dst_hd; num_heads unchanged.
314	
315	    Applied to the wo projection: weight (num_heads*src_hd, out_dim) becomes
316	    (num_heads*dst_hd, out_dim). The @transform_input_dim decorator passes
317	    1-D tensors (wo bias, which lives on the OUTPUT axis) through unchanged.
318	    """
319	    rest = t.shape[1:]
320	    t = t.reshape((num_heads, src_hd) + rest)
321	    pad = t.new_zeros((num_heads, dst_hd - src_hd) + rest)
322	    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=1).reshape((num_heads * dst_hd,) + rest)
323	
324	
325	@transform_output_dim
326	def _split_packed_vision_qkv(qkv):
327	    """Split HF vision QKV layout [Q | K | V] along output dim."""
328	    return tuple(x.contiguous() for x in qkv.chunk(3, dim=-1))
329	
330	
331	class Qwen3_5VisionModel(TextModel):
332	    """Vision sub-tree for Qwen3.5 VLM, rooted at ModelRoot.vision_model.
333	
334	    Subclasses ``TextModel`` purely to reuse the two-phase lifecycle
335	    (``__init__`` / ``bind_runtime``) and the ``_linear`` resolver helper;
336	    its ``cfg`` is the HF ``vision_config`` and ``_vocab_size`` is never used.
337	    """
338	
339	    def __init__(self, vision_cfg, *, resolver):
340	        super().__init__(vision_cfg, resolver=resolver)
341	
342	        self._vis_depth = int(vision_cfg.depth)
343	        self._vis_hidden = int(vision_cfg.hidden_size)
344	        self._vis_inter = int(vision_cfg.intermediate_size)
345	        self._vis_heads = int(vision_cfg.num_heads)
346	        self._vis_out_hidden = int(vision_cfg.out_hidden_size)
347	        self._vis_in_chans = int(vision_cfg.in_channels)
348	        self._vis_patch = int(vision_cfg.patch_size)
349	        self._vis_temporal = int(vision_cfg.temporal_patch_size)
350	        self._vis_pos_n = int(vision_cfg.num_position_embeddings)
351	        self._vis_spatial_merge = int(vision_cfg.spatial_merge_size)
352	        self._vis_norm_eps = 1e-6
353	
354	        # in_dim of the patcher when the Conv3d is reinterpreted as a
355	        # Linear over flattened patches: C * T * H * W.
356	        self._patch_in_dim = (self._vis_in_chans
357	                              * self._vis_temporal
358	                              * self._vis_patch
359	                              * self._vis_patch)
360	
361	    @staticmethod
362	    def _offset_pair(offset) -> tuple[int, int]:
363	        if isinstance(offset, torch.Tensor):
364	            values = offset.flatten().tolist()
365	        else:
366	            values = list(offset)
367	        if len(values) != 2:
368	            raise ValueError(f'Qwen3.5 ViT offset should contain 2 values, got {values!r}')
369	        return int(values[0]), int(values[1])
370	
371	    @staticmethod
372	    def _grid_thw(grid_thw) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
373	        if isinstance(grid_thw, torch.Tensor):
374	            values = grid_thw.flatten().tolist()
375	        else:
376	            values = list(grid_thw)
377	        if len(values) != 3:
378	            raise ValueError(f'Qwen3.5 ViT grid_thw should contain 3 values, got {values!r}')
379	        return int(values[0]), int(values[1]), int(values[2])
380	
381	    @staticmethod
382	    def _tm_tensor(tensor: torch.Tensor):
383	        if not isinstance(tensor, torch.Tensor):
384	            raise TypeError(f'Qwen3.5 ViT multimodal data should be a torch.Tensor, got {type(tensor).__name__}')
385	        return _tm.from_dlpack(tensor.contiguous())
386	
387	    def to_turbomind_multimodal(self, multimodal: list[dict[str, Any]]):
388	        """Convert Qwen3.5 VL preprocessor outputs to typed TurboMind input."""
389	        items = []
390	        for input_mm in multimodal:
391	            modality = input_mm.get('modality')
392	            if modality == Modality.IMAGE or modality == Modality.IMAGE.value:
393	                data = self._tm_tensor(input_mm['pixel_values'])
394	                grid_thw = self._grid_thw(input_mm['image_grid_thw'])
395	                tm_modality = _tm.multimodal.Modality.IMAGE
396	            elif modality == Modality.VIDEO or modality == Modality.VIDEO.value:
397	                data = self._tm_tensor(input_mm['pixel_values_videos'])
398	                grid_thw = self._grid_thw(input_mm['video_grid_thw'])
399	                tm_modality = _tm.multimodal.Modality.VIDEO
400	            else:
401	                raise ValueError(f'Qwen3.5 TurboMind does not support modality {modality!r}')
402	
403	            token_begin, token_end = self._offset_pair(input_mm['offset'])
404	            items.append(
405	                _tm.multimodal.Qwen3_5VitItem(
406	                    modality=tm_modality,
407	                    data=data,
408	                    token_begin=token_begin,
409	                    token_end=token_end,
410	                    grid_thw=grid_thw,
411	                ))
412	
413	        return _tm.multimodal.Qwen3_5VitInput(items)
414	
415	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
416	    # model() — build the vision sub-tree
417	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
418	
419	    def model(self, pfx):
420	        self._build_vision_model(pfx + 'model.visual')
421	
422	    def _restore_dtype(self, builder):
423	        """Builder.__init__ unconditionally overwrites cfg.data_type with the
424	        context's (text-engine) dtype.
425	
426	        The cfg is held by reference, so re-pinning it here propagates to every downstream _add_linear call on this
427	        builder, keeping the vision sub-tree on its native dtype.
428	        """
429	        builder.config.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
430	        return builder
431	
432	    def _build_vision_model(self, pfx):
433	        cfg = self._make_vision_root_cfg()
434	        root = self._restore_dtype(VisionModelBuilder(
435	            cfg, self._ctx,
436	            root_handles=self._root_handles,
437	            tp=self._model_tp))
438	
439	        root._add_tensor('pos_embed', (pfx + 'pos_embed').pop('weight'))
440	        root._add_linear('patch_embed', self._patch_embed(pfx + 'patch_embed.proj'))
441	
442	        root.blocks = self.vit_blocks(pfx + 'blocks')
443	
444	        root._add_linear('merger_fc1', self._linear(pfx + 'merger.linear_fc1'), SplitSide.OUTPUT)
445	        root._add_linear('merger_fc2', self._linear(pfx + 'merger.linear_fc2'), SplitSide.INPUT)
446	        root.merger_norm = self._layer_norm(pfx + 'merger.norm', dim=self._vis_hidden)
447	
448	        root.build()
449	
450	    def _make_vision_root_cfg(self):
451	        cfg = _tm.Qwen3_5VitConfig()
452	        cfg.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
453	        cfg.hidden_dim = self._vis_hidden
454	        cfg.out_hidden_dim = self._vis_out_hidden
455	        cfg.depth = self._vis_depth
456	        cfg.head_num = self._vis_heads
457	        cfg.intermediate_size = self._vis_inter
458	        cfg.patch_in_dim = self._patch_in_dim
459	        cfg.in_channels = self._vis_in_chans
460	        cfg.patch_size = self._vis_patch
461	        cfg.temporal_patch_size = self._vis_temporal
462	        cfg.num_position_embeddings = self._vis_pos_n
463	        cfg.spatial_merge_size = self._vis_spatial_merge
464	        cfg.norm_eps = self._vis_norm_eps
465	        return cfg
466	
467	    def _patch_embed(self, pfx):
468	        weight = pfx.pop('weight')
469	        weight = weight.reshape(weight.shape[0], -1).t().contiguous()
470	        tensors = {'weight': weight}
471	        if pfx.has('bias'):
472	            tensors['bias'] = pfx.pop('bias')
473	        return Linear(tensors=tensors, weight_format=TrivialFormat())
474	
475	    def vit_blocks(self, pfx):
476	        blocks = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)
477	
478	        for i, p in pfx.slices(0, self._vis_depth):
479	            blocks[i] = self.vit_block(p)
480	
481	        return blocks.build()
482	
483	    def vit_block(self, pfx):
484	        cfg = _tm.Qwen3_5VitBlockConfig()
485	        cfg.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
486	        cfg.hidden_dim = self._vis_hidden
487	        cfg.head_num = self._vis_heads
488	        cfg.intermediate_size = self._vis_inter
489	        cfg.norm_eps = self._vis_norm_eps
490	
491	        b = self._restore_dtype(Builder(cfg, self._ctx))
492	        b.tp = self._model_tp
493	
494	        b.norm1 = self._layer_norm(pfx + 'norm1', dim=self._vis_hidden)
495	        b.norm2 = self._layer_norm(pfx + 'norm2', dim=self._vis_hidden)
496	
497	        b.attention = self.vit_attn(pfx + 'attn')
498	        b._add_linear('mlp_fc1', self._linear(pfx + 'mlp.linear_fc1'), SplitSide.OUTPUT)
499	        b._add_linear('mlp_fc2', self._linear(pfx + 'mlp.linear_fc2'), SplitSide.INPUT)
500	        return b.build()
501	
502	    def _make_vision_attn_cfg(self):
503	        real_hd = self._vis_hidden // self._vis_heads
504	        padded_hd = _padded_vit_head_dim(real_hd)
505	        cfg = _tm.AttentionConfig()
506	        cfg.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
507	        cfg.hidden_dim = self._vis_hidden
508	        cfg.head_dim = padded_hd
509	        cfg.head_num = self._vis_heads
510	        cfg.kv_head_num = self._vis_heads
511	        cfg.window_size = 0
512	        cfg.causal = False
513	        # When we pad head_dim, the softmax scale must stay tied to the
514	        # real head_dim — the padded slice contributes zero to QK^T, so the
515	        # math is equivalent to head_dim=real_hd. Setting softmax_scale != 0
516	        # bypasses the runtime's `1/sqrt(attn.head_dim)` fallback.
517	        cfg.softmax_scale = (1.0 / math.sqrt(real_hd)
518	                             if padded_hd != real_hd else 0.0)
519	        return cfg
520	
521	    def vit_attn(self, pfx):
522	        cfg = self._make_vision_attn_cfg()
523	        real_hd = self._vis_hidden // self._vis_heads
524	        padded_hd = cfg.head_dim
525	        H = cfg.head_num
526	
527	        q, k, v = _split_packed_vision_qkv(self._linear(pfx + 'qkv'))
528	
529	        # Qwen3.5 ViT applies RoPE before invoking the attention kernel.
530	        # Reorder Q/K once at export time so the runtime can use the same
531	        # adjacent-pair RoPE layout as TurboMind's attention kernels.
532	        # RoPE is computed at the real head_dim regardless of padding.
533	        q = reorder_rotary_emb(q, real_hd, real_hd, resolver=self._resolver)
534	        k = reorder_rotary_emb(k, real_hd, real_hd, resolver=self._resolver)
535	
536	        proj = self._linear(pfx + 'proj')
537	
538	        # Only force TrivialFormat on the padded path; the native-head_dim
539	        # path keeps the existing quantized/non-quantized behavior intact.
540	        if padded_hd != real_hd:
541	            for ln, name in [(q, 'q'), (k, 'k'), (v, 'v'), (proj, 'proj')]:
542	                _assert_trivial(ln, name)
543	            pad_kwargs = dict(num_heads=H, src_hd=real_hd, dst_hd=padded_hd)
544	            q = _pad_head_dim_out(q, **pad_kwargs)
545	            k = _pad_head_dim_out(k, **pad_kwargs)
546	            v = _pad_head_dim_out(v, **pad_kwargs)
547	            proj = _pad_head_dim_in(proj, **pad_kwargs)
548	
549	        m = self._restore_dtype(
550	            AttentionBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._model_tp))
551	        m.add_qkv_proj(q, k, v)
552	        m.add_o_proj(proj)
553	        return m.build()
554	
555	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
556	    # Helper: build a LayerNorm child
557	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
558	
559	    def _layer_norm(self, pfx, *, dim: int):
560	        weight = pfx.pop('weight')
561	        bias = pfx.pop('bias') if pfx.has('bias') else None
562	        cfg = make_layer_norm_config(dim=dim,
563	                                     data_type=self._resolver.data_type,
564	                                     norm_eps=self._vis_norm_eps)
565	        m = self._restore_dtype(LayerNormBuilder(cfg, self._ctx))
566	        m.set_weight(weight, bias=bias)
567	        return m.build()
568	
569	
570	@INPUT_MODELS.register_module(name='qwen3_5-moe')
571	@INPUT_MODELS.register_module(name='qwen3_5')
572	class Qwen3_5Model:
573	    """Aggregate source model for Qwen3.5 checkpoints (text + optional
574	    vision)."""
575	
576	    _vision = True
577	
578	    def __init__(self, cfg: Qwen3_5Config | Qwen3_5MoeConfig, *, resolver,
579	                 vision_resolver=None,
580	                 language_model_only: bool = False):
581	        text_cfg = getattr(cfg, 'text_config', cfg)
582	        if text_cfg is None:
583	            raise ValueError(
584	                'Qwen3_5Model requires a checkpoint with text_config.')
585	        self.text_model = Qwen3_5TextModel(text_cfg, resolver=resolver)
586	
587	        vision_cfg = getattr(cfg, 'vision_config', None)
588	        if language_model_only or vision_cfg is None:
589	            self.vision_model = None
590	        else:
591	            self.vision_model = Qwen3_5VisionModel(
592	                vision_cfg, resolver=vision_resolver or resolver)
593	
594	    def bind_runtime(self, *, ctx, root_handles,
595	                     attn_tp, mlp_tp, model_tp):
596	        for m in (self.text_model, self.vision_model):
597	            if m is not None:
598	                m.bind_runtime(
599	                    ctx=ctx,
600	                    root_handles=root_handles,
601	                    attn_tp=attn_tp,
602	                    mlp_tp=mlp_tp,
603	                    model_tp=model_tp,
604	                )
605	
606	    @property
607	    def _vocab_size(self):
608	        return self.text_model.cfg.vocab_size
609	
610	    @property
611	    def _loader_mappings(self):
612	        return list(getattr(type(self.text_model), '_loader_mappings', []))
613	
614	    def to_turbomind_multimodal(self, multimodal: list[dict[str, Any]]):
615	        if self.vision_model is None:
616	            raise ValueError('Qwen3.5 TurboMind vision encoder is not available.')
617	        return self.vision_model.to_turbomind_multimodal(multimodal)
618	
619	    def model(self, pfx):
620	        # Text root child must be attached before the vision one, since both
621	        # use the shared root_handles.
622	        self.text_model.model(pfx)
623	        if self.vision_model is not None:
624	            self.vision_model.model(pfx)
625

file.content:
# Copyright (c) OpenMMLab. All rights reserved.
"""Qwen3.5 source models for the TurboMind pipeline.

This module hosts the full Qwen3.5 family in one place using composition
instead of inheritance (mirrors ``InternVLModel``):

* ``Qwen3_5TextModel`` -- text-only weight model (dense + linear-attn +
  optional MoE). Not registered on its own; used directly by the aggregate.
* ``Qwen3_5VisionModel`` -- the vision sub-tree rooted at
  ``ModelRoot.vision_model``.
* ``Qwen3_5Model`` -- a thin aggregate holding a ``text_model`` and an
  optional ``vision_model``, registered as ``qwen3_5`` / ``qwen3_5-moe``. It
  delegates the two-phase ``__init__`` / ``bind_runtime`` / ``model(pfx)``
  lifecycle to its children, and skips the vision encoder when
  ``language_model_only`` is set.

The patcher and position embedding are replicated across TP ranks. Vision
transformer blocks and merger linears shard with the model TP group.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import math
import re
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any

import _turbomind as _tm
import torch

from lmdeploy.vl.constants import Modality

from ..builders import (
    AttentionBuilder,
    Builder,
    DecoderLayerBuilder,
    DecoderLayerConfig,
    DeltaNetBuilder,
    FfnBuilder,
    LayerNormBuilder,
    ModuleListBuilder,
    ModuleListConfig,
    MoeBuilder,
    SplitSide,
    TextModelBuilder,
    VisionModelBuilder,
    _act_type_id,
    make_layer_norm_config,
)
from ..builders.attention import split_output_gate
from ..linear import Linear, transform_input_dim, transform_output_dim
from ..text_model import TextModel
from ..weight_format import TrivialFormat
from .base import INPUT_MODELS
from .utils import (
    make_attention_config,
    make_ffn_config,
    make_model_weight_config,
    make_moe_config,
    read_packed_moe_expert,
    reorder_rotary_emb,
)

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from transformers.models.qwen3_5.configuration_qwen3_5 import Qwen3_5Config, Qwen3_5TextConfig
    from transformers.models.qwen3_5_moe.configuration_qwen3_5_moe import Qwen3_5MoeConfig, Qwen3_5MoeTextConfig


def map_packed_qwen35_experts(name: str) -> str:
    """Map packed expert names to weight names so parameter.py can classify."""
    return re.sub(r'(mlp\.experts\.(?:gate_up|down)_proj)$', r'\1.weight', name)


def _center_norm(w):
    return 1.0 + w.float()


class Qwen3_5TextModel(TextModel):
    """Weight model for Qwen3.5 (dense + linear-attn + optional MoE)."""

    _loader_mappings = [map_packed_qwen35_experts]
    cfg: Qwen3_5TextConfig | Qwen3_5MoeTextConfig

    def __init__(self, cfg: Qwen3_5TextConfig | Qwen3_5MoeTextConfig, *, resolver):
        super().__init__(cfg, resolver=resolver)

        self._attn_cfg = make_attention_config(cfg)
        self._attn_cfg.output_gate = True
        self._attn_cfg.rope.mrope_interleaved = True

        self._n_experts = getattr(cfg, 'num_experts', 0)

        # ---- DeltaNet template ----
        ln_key_heads = cfg.linear_num_key_heads
        ln_val_heads = cfg.linear_num_value_heads
        ln_key_dim   = cfg.linear_key_head_dim
        ln_val_dim   = cfg.linear_value_head_dim

        self._dn_cfg = _tm.DeltaNetConfig()
        self._dn_cfg.hidden_dim      = self.cfg.hidden_size
        self._dn_cfg.num_k_heads     = ln_key_heads
        self._dn_cfg.num_v_heads     = ln_val_heads
        self._dn_cfg.key_head_dim    = ln_key_dim
        self._dn_cfg.value_head_dim  = ln_val_dim
        self._dn_cfg.d_conv          = cfg.linear_conv_kernel_dim or 4

        # ---- MoE template ----
        if self._n_experts > 0:
            self._moe_cfg = make_moe_config(
                cfg,
                experts_per_token=cfg.num_experts_per_tok)
            self._moe_cfg.expert_num = self._n_experts
            inter_size=cfg.moe_intermediate_size
        else:
            inter_size=cfg.intermediate_size

        # ---- FFN template ----
        self._ffn_cfg = make_ffn_config(
            cfg,
            act_type=_act_type_id('silu'), inter_size=inter_size)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # model() — same topology as old code
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def model(self, pfx):
        root_cfg = make_model_weight_config(self.cfg)
        builder = TextModelBuilder(
            root_cfg, self._ctx,
            root_handles=self._root_handles,
            tp=self._model_tp,
            vocab_size=self.cfg.vocab_size)
        builder.add_token_embeds(pfx.get('model.language_model.embed_tokens.weight'))
        builder.norm = self.norm(pfx + 'model.language_model.norm', _center_norm)
        lm_pfx = (pfx + 'model.language_model.embed_tokens'
                  if self.cfg.tie_word_embeddings
                  else pfx + 'lm_head')
        builder.add_lm_head(self._linear(lm_pfx))
        builder.layers = self.layers(pfx + 'model.language_model.layers')
        builder.build()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Attention / linear-attention factories
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def attn(self, pfx):
        q, k, v, o = [self._linear(pfx + f'{x}_proj') for x in 'qkvo']

        cfg = self._attn_cfg.clone()
        q, gate = split_output_gate(q, head_num=cfg.head_num)

        def reorder(x):
            return reorder_rotary_emb(x, cfg.head_dim, cfg.rope.dim, resolver=self._resolver)

        q, k = [reorder(x) for x in (q, k)]

        m = AttentionBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._attn_tp)

        m.add_qkv_proj(q, k, v, gate=gate)
        m.add_o_proj(o)

        m.q_norm = self.norm(pfx + 'q_norm',
                             lambda w: reorder(_center_norm(w)))
        m.k_norm = self.norm(pfx + 'k_norm',
                             lambda w: reorder(_center_norm(w)))

        return m.build()

    def linear_attn(self, pfx):
        cfg = self._dn_cfg.clone()
        builder = DeltaNetBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._attn_tp)

        builder.add_input_projections(
            in_proj_qkv=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_qkv'),
            in_proj_z=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_z'),
            in_proj_b=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_b'),
            in_proj_a=self._linear(pfx + 'in_proj_a'),
            out_proj=self._linear(pfx + 'out_proj'))
        builder.add_scalar_params(
            a_log=pfx.pop('A_log'),
            dt_bias=pfx.pop('dt_bias'))
        builder.add_conv1d(
            pfx.pop('conv1d.weight'))
        builder.norm = self.norm(pfx + 'norm')  # not zero-centered
        return builder.build()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # FFN / MoE factories
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def ffn(self, pfx, inter_size, is_expert=False):
        try:
            w1, w3, w2 = [self._linear(pfx + f'{x}_proj')
                          for x in ('gate', 'up', 'down')]
        except KeyError:
            return None

        cfg = self._ffn_cfg.clone()
        cfg.inter_size = inter_size
        cfg.is_expert  = is_expert

        m = FfnBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._mlp_tp)
        m.add_ffn(w1, w2, w3)
        return m.build()

    def moe(self, pfx):
        cfg = self._moe_cfg.clone()

        m = MoeBuilder(cfg, self._ctx)

        m.add_gate('gate', self._linear(pfx + 'gate'))

        experts_pfx = pfx + 'experts'
        experts = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)
        for e in range(self._n_experts):
            experts[e] = self._moe_expert_ffn(
                experts_pfx, e, self.cfg.moe_intermediate_size)
        m.experts = experts.build()

        m.add_gate('shared_gate', self._linear(pfx + 'shared_expert_gate'))
        shared = self.ffn(pfx + 'shared_expert', self.cfg.shared_expert_intermediate_size)

        return m.build(), shared

    def _packed_moe_ffn(self, experts_pfx, expert_idx, inter_size):
        w1, w2, w3 = read_packed_moe_expert(
            experts_pfx + 'gate_up_proj',
            experts_pfx + 'down_proj',
            expert_idx,
            resolver=self._resolver,
        )
        cfg = self._ffn_cfg.clone()
        cfg.inter_size = inter_size
        cfg.is_expert  = True
        m = FfnBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._mlp_tp)
        m.add_ffn(w1, w2, w3)
        return m.build()

    def _moe_expert_ffn(self, experts_pfx, expert_idx, inter_size):
        expert_pfx = experts_pfx + expert_idx
        return (self.ffn(expert_pfx, inter_size, is_expert=True)
                or self._packed_moe_ffn(experts_pfx, expert_idx, inter_size))

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # layers() — dispatch by layer type
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def layers(self, pfx):
        layers = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)
        for i, p in pfx.slices(0, self.cfg.num_hidden_layers):
            d = DecoderLayerBuilder(DecoderLayerConfig(), self._ctx)
            if self.cfg.layer_types[i] == 'linear_attention':
                d.linear_attn = self.linear_attn(p + 'linear_attn')
            else:
                d.attention = self.attn(p + 'self_attn')
            if self._n_experts > 0:
                d.moe_ffn, d.feed_forward = self.moe(p + 'mlp')
            else:
                d.feed_forward = self.ffn(p + 'mlp', self.cfg.intermediate_size)
            d.attention_norm = self.norm(p + 'input_layernorm', _center_norm)
            d.ffn_norm = self.norm(p + 'post_attention_layernorm', _center_norm)
            layers[i] = d.build()
        return layers.build()


# Explicit map: ViT head_dim -> kernel-supported head_dim. The attention
# kernel only ships {64,128,192,256,576} and impl_16816.h requires
# head_dim % 16 == 0, so head_dim=72 (Qwen3.5-27B ViT) is zero-padded
# per-head to 128. Other dims must be added here deliberately — we don't
# auto-round-up because the next-supported jump can be large.
_VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED = {
    64:  64,    # <=2B: native
    72:  128,   # >=9B: pad to nearest kernel-supported dim
}


def _padded_vit_head_dim(real_hd: int) -> int:
    if real_hd not in _VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED:
        raise NotImplementedError(
            f'Qwen3.5 ViT head_dim={real_hd} not supported; '
            f'known: {sorted(_VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED)}')
    return _VIT_HEAD_DIM_PADDED[real_hd]


def _assert_trivial(linear: Linear, name: str) -> None:
    """Padding only handles trivial weights for now.

    Callers must gate this on `padded_hd != real_hd` so quantized 2B-style paths stay untouched.
    """
    if not isinstance(linear.weight_format, TrivialFormat):
        raise NotImplementedError(
            f'ViT {name} weight is {type(linear.weight_format).__name__}; '
            f'head_dim padding currently supports TrivialFormat only '
            f'(dequant ViT before padding).')


@transform_output_dim
def _pad_head_dim_out(t: torch.Tensor, *, num_heads: int, src_hd: int,
                      dst_hd: int) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Pad each head's OUTPUT block from src_hd to dst_hd; num_heads unchanged.

    Applied to Q/K/V projections: weight (in_dim, num_heads*src_hd) becomes (in_dim, num_heads*dst_hd) with the new
    [src_hd, dst_hd) slice zeroed. The @transform_output_dim decorator handles bias by promoting it to 2-D and squeezing
    back, so the bias is padded the same way as weight.
    """
    rest = t.shape[:-1]
    t = t.reshape(rest + (num_heads, src_hd))
    pad = t.new_zeros(rest + (num_heads, dst_hd - src_hd))
    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=-1).reshape(rest + (num_heads * dst_hd,))


@transform_input_dim
def _pad_head_dim_in(t: torch.Tensor, *, num_heads: int, src_hd: int,
                     dst_hd: int) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Pad each head's INPUT block from src_hd to dst_hd; num_heads unchanged.

    Applied to the wo projection: weight (num_heads*src_hd, out_dim) becomes
    (num_heads*dst_hd, out_dim). The @transform_input_dim decorator passes
    1-D tensors (wo bias, which lives on the OUTPUT axis) through unchanged.
    """
    rest = t.shape[1:]
    t = t.reshape((num_heads, src_hd) + rest)
    pad = t.new_zeros((num_heads, dst_hd - src_hd) + rest)
    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=1).reshape((num_heads * dst_hd,) + rest)


@transform_output_dim
def _split_packed_vision_qkv(qkv):
    """Split HF vision QKV layout [Q | K | V] along output dim."""
    return tuple(x.contiguous() for x in qkv.chunk(3, dim=-1))


class Qwen3_5VisionModel(TextModel):
    """Vision sub-tree for Qwen3.5 VLM, rooted at ModelRoot.vision_model.

    Subclasses ``TextModel`` purely to reuse the two-phase lifecycle
    (``__init__`` / ``bind_runtime``) and the ``_linear`` resolver helper;
    its ``cfg`` is the HF ``vision_config`` and ``_vocab_size`` is never used.
    """

    def __init__(self, vision_cfg, *, resolver):
        super().__init__(vision_cfg, resolver=resolver)

        self._vis_depth = int(vision_cfg.depth)
        self._vis_hidden = int(vision_cfg.hidden_size)
        self._vis_inter = int(vision_cfg.intermediate_size)
        self._vis_heads = int(vision_cfg.num_heads)
        self._vis_out_hidden = int(vision_cfg.out_hidden_size)
        self._vis_in_chans = int(vision_cfg.in_channels)
        self._vis_patch = int(vision_cfg.patch_size)
        self._vis_temporal = int(vision_cfg.temporal_patch_size)
        self._vis_pos_n = int(vision_cfg.num_position_embeddings)
        self._vis_spatial_merge = int(vision_cfg.spatial_merge_size)
        self._vis_norm_eps = 1e-6

        # in_dim of the patcher when the Conv3d is reinterpreted as a
        # Linear over flattened patches: C * T * H * W.
        self._patch_in_dim = (self._vis_in_chans
                              * self._vis_temporal
                              * self._vis_patch
                              * self._vis_patch)

    @staticmethod
    def _offset_pair(offset) -> tuple[int, int]:
        if isinstance(offset, torch.Tensor):
            values = offset.flatten().tolist()
        else:
            values = list(offset)
        if len(values) != 2:
            raise ValueError(f'Qwen3.5 ViT offset should contain 2 values, got {values!r}')
        return int(values[0]), int(values[1])

    @staticmethod
    def _grid_thw(grid_thw) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
        if isinstance(grid_thw, torch.Tensor):
            values = grid_thw.flatten().tolist()
        else:
            values = list(grid_thw)
        if len(values) != 3:
            raise ValueError(f'Qwen3.5 ViT grid_thw should contain 3 values, got {values!r}')
        return int(values[0]), int(values[1]), int(values[2])

    @staticmethod
    def _tm_tensor(tensor: torch.Tensor):
        if not isinstance(tensor, torch.Tensor):
            raise TypeError(f'Qwen3.5 ViT multimodal data should be a torch.Tensor, got {type(tensor).__name__}')
        return _tm.from_dlpack(tensor.contiguous())

    def to_turbomind_multimodal(self, multimodal: list[dict[str, Any]]):
        """Convert Qwen3.5 VL preprocessor outputs to typed TurboMind input."""
        items = []
        for input_mm in multimodal:
            modality = input_mm.get('modality')
            if modality == Modality.IMAGE or modality == Modality.IMAGE.value:
                data = self._tm_tensor(input_mm['pixel_values'])
                grid_thw = self._grid_thw(input_mm['image_grid_thw'])
                tm_modality = _tm.multimodal.Modality.IMAGE
            elif modality == Modality.VIDEO or modality == Modality.VIDEO.value:
                data = self._tm_tensor(input_mm['pixel_values_videos'])
                grid_thw = self._grid_thw(input_mm['video_grid_thw'])
                tm_modality = _tm.multimodal.Modality.VIDEO
            else:
                raise ValueError(f'Qwen3.5 TurboMind does not support modality {modality!r}')

            token_begin, token_end = self._offset_pair(input_mm['offset'])
            items.append(
                _tm.multimodal.Qwen3_5VitItem(
                    modality=tm_modality,
                    data=data,
                    token_begin=token_begin,
                    token_end=token_end,
                    grid_thw=grid_thw,
                ))

        return _tm.multimodal.Qwen3_5VitInput(items)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # model() — build the vision sub-tree
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def model(self, pfx):
        self._build_vision_model(pfx + 'model.visual')

    def _restore_dtype(self, builder):
        """Builder.__init__ unconditionally overwrites cfg.data_type with the
        context's (text-engine) dtype.

        The cfg is held by reference, so re-pinning it here propagates to every downstream _add_linear call on this
        builder, keeping the vision sub-tree on its native dtype.
        """
        builder.config.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
        return builder

    def _build_vision_model(self, pfx):
        cfg = self._make_vision_root_cfg()
        root = self._restore_dtype(VisionModelBuilder(
            cfg, self._ctx,
            root_handles=self._root_handles,
            tp=self._model_tp))

        root._add_tensor('pos_embed', (pfx + 'pos_embed').pop('weight'))
        root._add_linear('patch_embed', self._patch_embed(pfx + 'patch_embed.proj'))

        root.blocks = self.vit_blocks(pfx + 'blocks')

        root._add_linear('merger_fc1', self._linear(pfx + 'merger.linear_fc1'), SplitSide.OUTPUT)
        root._add_linear('merger_fc2', self._linear(pfx + 'merger.linear_fc2'), SplitSide.INPUT)
        root.merger_norm = self._layer_norm(pfx + 'merger.norm', dim=self._vis_hidden)

        root.build()

    def _make_vision_root_cfg(self):
        cfg = _tm.Qwen3_5VitConfig()
        cfg.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
        cfg.hidden_dim = self._vis_hidden
        cfg.out_hidden_dim = self._vis_out_hidden
        cfg.depth = self._vis_depth
        cfg.head_num = self._vis_heads
        cfg.intermediate_size = self._vis_inter
        cfg.patch_in_dim = self._patch_in_dim
        cfg.in_channels = self._vis_in_chans
        cfg.patch_size = self._vis_patch
        cfg.temporal_patch_size = self._vis_temporal
        cfg.num_position_embeddings = self._vis_pos_n
        cfg.spatial_merge_size = self._vis_spatial_merge
        cfg.norm_eps = self._vis_norm_eps
        return cfg

    def _patch_embed(self, pfx):
        weight = pfx.pop('weight')
        weight = weight.reshape(weight.shape[0], -1).t().contiguous()
        tensors = {'weight': weight}
        if pfx.has('bias'):
            tensors['bias'] = pfx.pop('bias')
        return Linear(tensors=tensors, weight_format=TrivialFormat())

    def vit_blocks(self, pfx):
        blocks = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)

        for i, p in pfx.slices(0, self._vis_depth):
            blocks[i] = self.vit_block(p)

        return blocks.build()

    def vit_block(self, pfx):
        cfg = _tm.Qwen3_5VitBlockConfig()
        cfg.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
        cfg.hidden_dim = self._vis_hidden
        cfg.head_num = self._vis_heads
        cfg.intermediate_size = self._vis_inter
        cfg.norm_eps = self._vis_norm_eps

        b = self._restore_dtype(Builder(cfg, self._ctx))
        b.tp = self._model_tp

        b.norm1 = self._layer_norm(pfx + 'norm1', dim=self._vis_hidden)
        b.norm2 = self._layer_norm(pfx + 'norm2', dim=self._vis_hidden)

        b.attention = self.vit_attn(pfx + 'attn')
        b._add_linear('mlp_fc1', self._linear(pfx + 'mlp.linear_fc1'), SplitSide.OUTPUT)
        b._add_linear('mlp_fc2', self._linear(pfx + 'mlp.linear_fc2'), SplitSide.INPUT)
        return b.build()

    def _make_vision_attn_cfg(self):
        real_hd = self._vis_hidden // self._vis_heads
        padded_hd = _padded_vit_head_dim(real_hd)
        cfg = _tm.AttentionConfig()
        cfg.data_type = self._resolver.data_type
        cfg.hidden_dim = self._vis_hidden
        cfg.head_dim = padded_hd
        cfg.head_num = self._vis_heads
        cfg.kv_head_num = self._vis_heads
        cfg.window_size = 0
        cfg.causal = False
        # When we pad head_dim, the softmax scale must stay tied to the
        # real head_dim — the padded slice contributes zero to QK^T, so the
        # math is equivalent to head_dim=real_hd. Setting softmax_scale != 0
        # bypasses the runtime's `1/sqrt(attn.head_dim)` fallback.
        cfg.softmax_scale = (1.0 / math.sqrt(real_hd)
                             if padded_hd != real_hd else 0.0)
        return cfg

    def vit_attn(self, pfx):
        cfg = self._make_vision_attn_cfg()
        real_hd = self._vis_hidden // self._vis_heads
        padded_hd = cfg.head_dim
        H = cfg.head_num

        q, k, v = _split_packed_vision_qkv(self._linear(pfx + 'qkv'))

        # Qwen3.5 ViT applies RoPE before invoking the attention kernel.
        # Reorder Q/K once at export time so the runtime can use the same
        # adjacent-pair RoPE layout as TurboMind's attention kernels.
        # RoPE is computed at the real head_dim regardless of padding.
        q = reorder_rotary_emb(q, real_hd, real_hd, resolver=self._resolver)
        k = reorder_rotary_emb(k, real_hd, real_hd, resolver=self._resolver)

        proj = self._linear(pfx + 'proj')

        # Only force TrivialFormat on the padded path; the native-head_dim
        # path keeps the existing quantized/non-quantized behavior intact.
        if padded_hd != real_hd:
            for ln, name in [(q, 'q'), (k, 'k'), (v, 'v'), (proj, 'proj')]:
                _assert_trivial(ln, name)
            pad_kwargs = dict(num_heads=H, src_hd=real_hd, dst_hd=padded_hd)
            q = _pad_head_dim_out(q, **pad_kwargs)
            k = _pad_head_dim_out(k, **pad_kwargs)
            v = _pad_head_dim_out(v, **pad_kwargs)
            proj = _pad_head_dim_in(proj, **pad_kwargs)

        m = self._restore_dtype(
            AttentionBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._model_tp))
        m.add_qkv_proj(q, k, v)
        m.add_o_proj(proj)
        return m.build()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Helper: build a LayerNorm child
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _layer_norm(self, pfx, *, dim: int):
        weight = pfx.pop('weight')
        bias = pfx.pop('bias') if pfx.has('bias') else None
        cfg = make_layer_norm_config(dim=dim,
                                     data_type=self._resolver.data_type,
                                     norm_eps=self._vis_norm_eps)
        m = self._restore_dtype(LayerNormBuilder(cfg, self._ctx))
        m.set_weight(weight, bias=bias)
        return m.build()


@INPUT_MODELS.register_module(name='qwen3_5-moe')
@INPUT_MODELS.register_module(name='qwen3_5')
class Qwen3_5Model:
    """Aggregate source model for Qwen3.5 checkpoints (text + optional
    vision)."""

    _vision = True

    def __init__(self, cfg: Qwen3_5Config | Qwen3_5MoeConfig, *, resolver,
                 vision_resolver=None,
                 language_model_only: bool = False):
        text_cfg = getattr(cfg, 'text_config', cfg)
        if text_cfg is None:
            raise ValueError(
                'Qwen3_5Model requires a checkpoint with text_config.')
        self.text_model = Qwen3_5TextModel(text_cfg, resolver=resolver)

        vision_cfg = getattr(cfg, 'vision_config', None)
        if language_model_only or vision_cfg is None:
            self.vision_model = None
        else:
            self.vision_model = Qwen3_5VisionModel(
                vision_cfg, resolver=vision_resolver or resolver)

    def bind_runtime(self, *, ctx, root_handles,
                     attn_tp, mlp_tp, model_tp):
        for m in (self.text_model, self.vision_model):
            if m is not None:
                m.bind_runtime(
                    ctx=ctx,
                    root_handles=root_handles,
                    attn_tp=attn_tp,
                    mlp_tp=mlp_tp,
                    model_tp=model_tp,
                )

    @property
    def _vocab_size(self):
        return self.text_model.cfg.vocab_size

    @property
    def _loader_mappings(self):
        return list(getattr(type(self.text_model), '_loader_mappings', []))

    def to_turbomind_multimodal(self, multimodal: list[dict[str, Any]]):
        if self.vision_model is None:
            raise ValueError('Qwen3.5 TurboMind vision encoder is not available.')
        return self.vision_model.to_turbomind_multimodal(multimodal)

    def model(self, pfx):
        # Text root child must be attached before the vision one, since both
        # use the shared root_handles.
        self.text_model.model(pfx)
        if self.vision_model is not None:
            self.vision_model.model(pfx)

1	# Copyright (c) OpenMMLab. All rights reserved.
2	"""Linear weight bundle and composable dimension operations.
3	
4	Two weight types flow through the TurboMind weight loading pipeline:
5	
6	- ``Linear`` -- a bundle of tensors for a single linear layer (weight +
7	  optional scales, zeros, bias).
8	- Raw ``torch.Tensor`` -- everything else (norms, embeddings, scalars).
9	
10	**concat_out_dim** joins ``Linear`` bundles along the output
11	dimension, handling all component tensors correctly regardless of
12	quantization-induced dimension scaling.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import functools
18	import inspect
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
21	
22	import torch
23	from torch import Tensor
24	
25	if TYPE_CHECKING:
26	    from .weight_format import WeightFormat
27	
28	
29	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	# Linear dataclass with methods
31	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
32	
33	
34	@dataclass
35	class Linear:
36	    """Bundle of tensors for a single linear layer.
37	
38	    ``tensors`` maps a closed-set TM weight kind (e.g. ``"weight"``,
39	    ``"scales"``, ``"zeros"``, ``"bias"``, ``"qweight"``) to the actual
40	    tensor.
41	
42	    **Layout contract**: all ``Linear`` objects are in TM layout with
43	    axis 0 as the input dimension and axis -1 as the output dimension.
44	    ``commit_linear`` assumes this layout and does not re-transpose.
45	    1-D tensors (e.g. bias) only have an output dimension (axis 0).
46	    """
47	
48	    tensors: dict[str, Tensor]
49	    weight_format: WeightFormat = field(compare=False, repr=False)
50	
51	
52	def concat_out_dim(xs: list[Linear]) -> Linear:
53	    """Concatenate along output dim."""
54	    first = xs[0]
55	    result: dict[str, Tensor] = {}
56	    for kind in first.tensors:
57	        t = first.tensors[kind]
58	        result[kind] = torch.cat([x.tensors[kind] for x in xs], dim=t.dim() - 1)
59	    wfmts = {x.weight_format for x in xs}
60	    assert len(wfmts) == 1, (
61	        'concat_out_dim requires uniform weight_format; '
62	        'call dequant_mixed first if formats differ.')
63	    return Linear(tensors=result,
64	                  weight_format=next(iter(wfmts)))
65	
66	
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	# Format / compatibility utilities
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	
71	
72	def _dequant_linear(linear: Linear, *, data_type) -> Linear:
73	    """Dequantize a quantized Linear to trivial.
74	
75	    ``TrivialFormat.dequant`` is identity, so already-trivial inputs round-trip
76	    safely.  ``AWQFormat.dequant``, ``CompressedTensorFormat.dequant`` and
77	    ``FP8Format.dequant`` do real work. GPTQ / MXFP4 inherit the base-class
78	    ``NotImplementedError`` — calling ``_dequant_linear`` on one of those is a
79	    broken-fusion-group configuration, and the raise names it at the call site.
80	    """
81	    from .weight_format import TrivialFormat
82	
83	    fmt = linear.weight_format
84	    new_tensors = fmt.dequant(linear.tensors, data_type)
85	    trivial = TrivialFormat()
86	    return Linear(tensors=new_tensors, weight_format=trivial)
87	
88	
89	def dequant_mixed(*linears: Linear | None, data_type) -> tuple[Linear | None, ...]:
90	    """Dequantize linears to a common trivial format when formats differ.
91	
92	    Trivial inputs round-trip safely through ``_dequant_linear``.
93	    None args pass through unchanged.
94	    """
95	    formats = {l.weight_format.name for l in linears if l is not None}
96	    if len(formats) <= 1:
97	        return linears
98	    return tuple(
99	        _dequant_linear(l, data_type=data_type) if l is not None else l
100	        for l in linears
101	    )
102	
103	
104	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	# Linear-level transform decorators
106	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107	
108	
109	def transform_output_dim(fn):
110	    """Decorator that lifts a tensor-level transform to Linear-level.
111	
112	    For output-dim operations: 1-D tensors (bias) are unsqueezed to 2-D
113	    before calling *fn*, then squeezed back.  Convention: args that are
114	    ``Linear`` instances are treated as tensor inputs; all other args pass
115	    through unchanged.  Return type is detected at runtime:
116	    ``Tensor`` -> single ``Linear``, ``tuple`` -> tuple of ``Linear`` objects.
117	    """
118	    sig = inspect.signature(fn)
119	
120	    @functools.wraps(fn)
121	    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
122	        bound = sig.bind(*args, **kwargs)
123	        bound.apply_defaults()
124	
125	        first = next(
126	            v for v in bound.arguments.values() if isinstance(v, Linear)
127	        )
128	        out_buckets = None
129	
130	        for kind in first.tensors:
131	            was_1d = False
132	            fn_kwargs = {}
133	
134	            for name, val in bound.arguments.items():
135	                if isinstance(val, Linear):
136	                    t = val.tensors[kind]
137	                    if t.dim() == 1:
138	                        was_1d = True
139	                        t = t.unsqueeze(0)
140	                    fn_kwargs[name] = t
141	                else:
142	                    fn_kwargs[name] = val
143	
144	            result = fn(**fn_kwargs)
145	            if not isinstance(result, tuple):
146	                result = (result,)
147	            if out_buckets is None:
148	                out_buckets = [{} for _ in result]
149	            for i, item in enumerate(result):
150	                out_buckets[i][kind] = item.squeeze(0) if was_1d else item
151	
152	        outputs = tuple(
153	            Linear(ts, weight_format=first.weight_format) for ts in out_buckets
154	        )
155	        return outputs if len(outputs) > 1 else outputs[0]
156	
157	    return wrapper
158	
159	
160	def transform_input_dim(fn):
161	    """Decorator that lifts a tensor-level transform to Linear-level.
162	
163	    For input-dim operations: 1-D tensors (bias) have no input dimension
164	    and are **passed through unchanged**.  The inner function only ever
165	    sees 2-D tensors for each kind.  For multi-output functions, 1-D
166	    tensors are duplicated into every output bucket.
167	    """
168	    sig = inspect.signature(fn)
169	
170	    @functools.wraps(fn)
171	    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
172	        bound = sig.bind(*args, **kwargs)
173	        bound.apply_defaults()
174	
175	        first = next(
176	            v for v in bound.arguments.values() if isinstance(v, Linear)
177	        )
178	        out_buckets = None
179	        deferred_1d: list[str] = []
180	
181	        for kind in first.tensors:
182	            fn_kwargs = {}
183	            is_1d = False
184	
185	            for name, val in bound.arguments.items():
186	                if isinstance(val, Linear):
187	                    t = val.tensors[kind]
188	                    if t.dim() < 2:
189	                        is_1d = True
190	                        break
191	                    fn_kwargs[name] = t
192	                else:
193	                    fn_kwargs[name] = val
194	
195	            if is_1d:
196	                deferred_1d.append(kind)
197	                continue
198	
199	            result = fn(**fn_kwargs)
200	            if not isinstance(result, tuple):
201	                result = (result,)
202	            if out_buckets is None:
203	                out_buckets = [{} for _ in result]
204	            for i, item in enumerate(result):
205	                out_buckets[i][kind] = item
206	
207	        if out_buckets is None:
208	            out_buckets = [{}]
209	        for kind in deferred_1d:
210	            for bucket in out_buckets:
211	                bucket[kind] = first.tensors[kind]
212	
213	        outputs = tuple(
214	            Linear(ts, weight_format=first.weight_format) for ts in out_buckets
215	        )
216	        return outputs if len(outputs) > 1 else outputs[0]
217	
218	    return wrapper
219	
220	
221	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
222	# Group-based padding
223	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
224	
225	
226	@transform_output_dim
227	def pad_output_groups(t: torch.Tensor, *, src_groups: int,
228	                      dst_groups: int) -> torch.Tensor:
229	    """Pad output dim by src_groups → dst_groups, viewing it as (groups,
230	    -1)."""
231	    t = t.reshape(t.shape[:-1] + (src_groups, -1))
232	    pad = t.new_zeros(t.shape[:-2] + (dst_groups - src_groups, t.shape[-1]))
233	    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=-2).reshape(t.shape[:-2] + (-1,))
234	
235	
236	@transform_input_dim
237	def pad_input_groups(t: torch.Tensor, *, src_groups: int,
238	                     dst_groups: int) -> torch.Tensor:
239	    """Pad input dim by src_groups → dst_groups, viewing it as (groups, -1)."""
240	    t = t.reshape((src_groups, -1) + t.shape[1:])
241	    block = t.shape[1]
242	    pad = t.new_zeros((dst_groups - src_groups, block) + t.shape[2:])
243	    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=0).reshape((dst_groups * block,) + t.shape[2:])
244	
245	
246	def _round_up(src_groups: int, div: int) -> int:
247	    """Round *src_groups* up to the nearest multiple of *div*."""
248	    return ((src_groups + div - 1) // div) * div
249	
250	
251	def round_up_output_groups(linear: Linear, groups: int,
252	                           div: int) -> Linear:
253	    """Pad output-dim groups to ``round_up(groups, div)``."""
254	    dst = _round_up(groups, div)
255	    if dst == groups:
256	        return linear
257	    return pad_output_groups(linear, src_groups=groups, dst_groups=dst)
258	
259	
260	def round_up_input_groups(linear: Linear, groups: int,
261	                          div: int) -> Linear:
262	    """Pad input-dim groups to ``round_up(groups, div)``."""
263	    dst = _round_up(groups, div)
264	    if dst == groups:
265	        return linear
266	    return pad_input_groups(linear, src_groups=groups, dst_groups=dst)

# Copyright (c) OpenMMLab. All rights reserved.
"""Linear weight bundle and composable dimension operations.

Two weight types flow through the TurboMind weight loading pipeline:

- ``Linear`` -- a bundle of tensors for a single linear layer (weight +
  optional scales, zeros, bias).
- Raw ``torch.Tensor`` -- everything else (norms, embeddings, scalars).

**concat_out_dim** joins ``Linear`` bundles along the output
dimension, handling all component tensors correctly regardless of
quantization-induced dimension scaling.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import functools
import inspect
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

import torch
from torch import Tensor

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .weight_format import WeightFormat


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Linear dataclass with methods
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@dataclass
class Linear:
    """Bundle of tensors for a single linear layer.

    ``tensors`` maps a closed-set TM weight kind (e.g. ``"weight"``,
    ``"scales"``, ``"zeros"``, ``"bias"``, ``"qweight"``) to the actual
    tensor.

    **Layout contract**: all ``Linear`` objects are in TM layout with
    axis 0 as the input dimension and axis -1 as the output dimension.
    ``commit_linear`` assumes this layout and does not re-transpose.
    1-D tensors (e.g. bias) only have an output dimension (axis 0).
    """

    tensors: dict[str, Tensor]
    weight_format: WeightFormat = field(compare=False, repr=False)


def concat_out_dim(xs: list[Linear]) -> Linear:
    """Concatenate along output dim."""
    first = xs[0]
    result: dict[str, Tensor] = {}
    for kind in first.tensors:
        t = first.tensors[kind]
        result[kind] = torch.cat([x.tensors[kind] for x in xs], dim=t.dim() - 1)
    wfmts = {x.weight_format for x in xs}
    assert len(wfmts) == 1, (
        'concat_out_dim requires uniform weight_format; '
        'call dequant_mixed first if formats differ.')
    return Linear(tensors=result,
                  weight_format=next(iter(wfmts)))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Format / compatibility utilities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _dequant_linear(linear: Linear, *, data_type) -> Linear:
    """Dequantize a quantized Linear to trivial.

    ``TrivialFormat.dequant`` is identity, so already-trivial inputs round-trip
    safely.  ``AWQFormat.dequant``, ``CompressedTensorFormat.dequant`` and
    ``FP8Format.dequant`` do real work. GPTQ / MXFP4 inherit the base-class
    ``NotImplementedError`` — calling ``_dequant_linear`` on one of those is a
    broken-fusion-group configuration, and the raise names it at the call site.
    """
    from .weight_format import TrivialFormat

    fmt = linear.weight_format
    new_tensors = fmt.dequant(linear.tensors, data_type)
    trivial = TrivialFormat()
    return Linear(tensors=new_tensors, weight_format=trivial)


def dequant_mixed(*linears: Linear | None, data_type) -> tuple[Linear | None, ...]:
    """Dequantize linears to a common trivial format when formats differ.

    Trivial inputs round-trip safely through ``_dequant_linear``.
    None args pass through unchanged.
    """
    formats = {l.weight_format.name for l in linears if l is not None}
    if len(formats) <= 1:
        return linears
    return tuple(
        _dequant_linear(l, data_type=data_type) if l is not None else l
        for l in linears
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Linear-level transform decorators
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def transform_output_dim(fn):
    """Decorator that lifts a tensor-level transform to Linear-level.

    For output-dim operations: 1-D tensors (bias) are unsqueezed to 2-D
    before calling *fn*, then squeezed back.  Convention: args that are
    ``Linear`` instances are treated as tensor inputs; all other args pass
    through unchanged.  Return type is detected at runtime:
    ``Tensor`` -> single ``Linear``, ``tuple`` -> tuple of ``Linear`` objects.
    """
    sig = inspect.signature(fn)

    @functools.wraps(fn)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        bound = sig.bind(*args, **kwargs)
        bound.apply_defaults()

        first = next(
            v for v in bound.arguments.values() if isinstance(v, Linear)
        )
        out_buckets = None

        for kind in first.tensors:
            was_1d = False
            fn_kwargs = {}

            for name, val in bound.arguments.items():
                if isinstance(val, Linear):
                    t = val.tensors[kind]
                    if t.dim() == 1:
                        was_1d = True
                        t = t.unsqueeze(0)
                    fn_kwargs[name] = t
                else:
                    fn_kwargs[name] = val

            result = fn(**fn_kwargs)
            if not isinstance(result, tuple):
                result = (result,)
            if out_buckets is None:
                out_buckets = [{} for _ in result]
            for i, item in enumerate(result):
                out_buckets[i][kind] = item.squeeze(0) if was_1d else item

        outputs = tuple(
            Linear(ts, weight_format=first.weight_format) for ts in out_buckets
        )
        return outputs if len(outputs) > 1 else outputs[0]

    return wrapper


def transform_input_dim(fn):
    """Decorator that lifts a tensor-level transform to Linear-level.

    For input-dim operations: 1-D tensors (bias) have no input dimension
    and are **passed through unchanged**.  The inner function only ever
    sees 2-D tensors for each kind.  For multi-output functions, 1-D
    tensors are duplicated into every output bucket.
    """
    sig = inspect.signature(fn)

    @functools.wraps(fn)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        bound = sig.bind(*args, **kwargs)
        bound.apply_defaults()

        first = next(
            v for v in bound.arguments.values() if isinstance(v, Linear)
        )
        out_buckets = None
        deferred_1d: list[str] = []

        for kind in first.tensors:
            fn_kwargs = {}
            is_1d = False

            for name, val in bound.arguments.items():
                if isinstance(val, Linear):
                    t = val.tensors[kind]
                    if t.dim() < 2:
                        is_1d = True
                        break
                    fn_kwargs[name] = t
                else:
                    fn_kwargs[name] = val

            if is_1d:
                deferred_1d.append(kind)
                continue

            result = fn(**fn_kwargs)
            if not isinstance(result, tuple):
                result = (result,)
            if out_buckets is None:
                out_buckets = [{} for _ in result]
            for i, item in enumerate(result):
                out_buckets[i][kind] = item

        if out_buckets is None:
            out_buckets = [{}]
        for kind in deferred_1d:
            for bucket in out_buckets:
                bucket[kind] = first.tensors[kind]

        outputs = tuple(
            Linear(ts, weight_format=first.weight_format) for ts in out_buckets
        )
        return outputs if len(outputs) > 1 else outputs[0]

    return wrapper


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Group-based padding
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@transform_output_dim
def pad_output_groups(t: torch.Tensor, *, src_groups: int,
                      dst_groups: int) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Pad output dim by src_groups → dst_groups, viewing it as (groups,
    -1)."""
    t = t.reshape(t.shape[:-1] + (src_groups, -1))
    pad = t.new_zeros(t.shape[:-2] + (dst_groups - src_groups, t.shape[-1]))
    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=-2).reshape(t.shape[:-2] + (-1,))


@transform_input_dim
def pad_input_groups(t: torch.Tensor, *, src_groups: int,
                     dst_groups: int) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Pad input dim by src_groups → dst_groups, viewing it as (groups, -1)."""
    t = t.reshape((src_groups, -1) + t.shape[1:])
    block = t.shape[1]
    pad = t.new_zeros((dst_groups - src_groups, block) + t.shape[2:])
    return torch.cat([t, pad], dim=0).reshape((dst_groups * block,) + t.shape[2:])


def _round_up(src_groups: int, div: int) -> int:
    """Round *src_groups* up to the nearest multiple of *div*."""
    return ((src_groups + div - 1) // div) * div


def round_up_output_groups(linear: Linear, groups: int,
                           div: int) -> Linear:
    """Pad output-dim groups to ``round_up(groups, div)``."""
    dst = _round_up(groups, div)
    if dst == groups:
        return linear
    return pad_output_groups(linear, src_groups=groups, dst_groups=dst)


def round_up_input_groups(linear: Linear, groups: int,
                          div: int) -> Linear:
    """Pad input-dim groups to ``round_up(groups, div)``."""
    dst = _round_up(groups, div)
    if dst == groups:
        return linear
    return pad_input_groups(linear, src_groups=groups, dst_groups=dst)

1	# Copyright (c) OpenMMLab. All rights reserved.
2	"""GLM-4 MoE Lite (GLM-4.7-Flash) TextModel for the new pipeline."""
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
6	
7	import _turbomind as _tm
8	
9	if TYPE_CHECKING:
10	    from transformers import Glm4MoeLiteConfig
11	
12	from ..builders import (
13	    DecoderLayerBuilder,
14	    DecoderLayerConfig,
15	    FfnBuilder,
16	    MLABuilder,
17	    ModuleListBuilder,
18	    ModuleListConfig,
19	    MoeBuilder,
20	    TextModelBuilder,
21	    _act_type_id,
22	)
23	from ..text_model import TextModel
24	from .base import INPUT_MODELS
25	from .utils import (
26	    make_mla_config,
27	    make_model_weight_config,
28	    make_moe_config,
29	)
30	
31	
32	@INPUT_MODELS.register_module(name='glm4-moe-lite')
33	class Glm4MoeLiteModel(TextModel):
34	    """Weight model for GLM-4 MoE Lite (e.g. GLM-4.7-Flash)."""
35	
36	    cfg: Glm4MoeLiteConfig
37	
38	    def __init__(self, cfg: Glm4MoeLiteConfig, *, resolver):
39	        super().__init__(cfg, resolver=resolver)
40	
41	        self._attn_cfg = make_mla_config(cfg)
42	
43	        # ---- FFN template ----
44	        self._ffn_cfg = _tm.FfnConfig()
45	        self._ffn_cfg.hidden_dim = self.cfg.hidden_size
46	        self._ffn_cfg.act_type   = _act_type_id('silu')
47	
48	        # ---- MoE template (GLM-specific: noaux_tc + sigmoid) ----
49	        if cfg.n_routed_experts > 0:
50	            self._moe_cfg = make_moe_config(
51	                cfg,
52	                experts_per_token=cfg.num_experts_per_tok,
53	                topk_method='noaux_tc',
54	                scoring_func='sigmoid',
55	                routed_scale=cfg.routed_scaling_factor,
56	                topk_group=cfg.topk_group,
57	                n_group=cfg.n_group)
58	            self._moe_cfg.expert_num = cfg.n_routed_experts
59	
60	        self._tune_layer_num = 2  # GLM-MoE recommends tuning 2 layers
61	
62	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
63	    # model()
64	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
65	
66	    def model(self, pfx):
67	        root_cfg = make_model_weight_config(self.cfg)
68	        builder = TextModelBuilder(
69	            root_cfg, self._ctx,
70	            root_handles=self._root_handles,
71	            tp=self._model_tp,
72	            vocab_size=self.cfg.vocab_size)
73	        builder.add_token_embeds(pfx.get('model.embed_tokens.weight'))
74	        builder.norm = self.norm(pfx + 'model.norm')
75	        builder.add_lm_head(self._linear(pfx + 'lm_head'))  # GLM: never tied
76	        builder.layers = self.layers(pfx + 'model.layers')
77	        builder.build()
78	
79	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
80	    # MLA attention (uses MLABuilder + self._attn_cfg clone)
81	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
82	
83	    def attn(self, pfx):
84	        cfg = self._attn_cfg.clone()
85	        m = MLABuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._attn_tp)
86	
87	        q_b = (self._linear(pfx + 'q_b_proj', optional=True) or
88	               self._linear(pfx + 'q_proj'))
89	        m.add_projections(
90	            q_a_proj=self._linear(pfx + 'q_a_proj'),
91	            q_b_proj=q_b,
92	            kv_a_proj=self._linear(pfx + 'kv_a_proj_with_mqa'),
93	            kv_b_proj=self._linear(pfx + 'kv_b_proj'),
94	            wo=self._linear(pfx + 'o_proj'),
95	        )
96	        m.q_a_layernorm  = self.norm(pfx + 'q_a_layernorm')
97	        m.kv_a_layernorm = self.norm(pfx + 'kv_a_layernorm')
98	        return m.build()
99	
100	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
101	    # FFN / MoE factories
102	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
103	
104	    def ffn(self, pfx, inter_size, is_expert=False):
105	        w1, w3, w2 = [self._linear(pfx + f'{x}_proj') for x in ('gate', 'up', 'down')]
106	
107	        cfg = self._ffn_cfg.clone()
108	        cfg.inter_size = inter_size
109	        cfg.is_expert  = is_expert
110	
111	        m = FfnBuilder(cfg, self._ctx, tp=self._mlp_tp)
112	        m.add_ffn(w1, w2, w3)
113	        return m.build()
114	
115	    def moe(self, pfx):
116	        cfg = self._moe_cfg.clone()
117	
118	        m = MoeBuilder(cfg, self._ctx)
119	
120	        m.add_gate('gate', self._linear(pfx + 'gate'))
121	
122	        correction = pfx.pop('gate.e_score_correction_bias')
123	        m.add_param('score_correction_bias', correction)
124	
125	        experts = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)
126	        for e in range(cfg.expert_num):
127	            experts[e] = self.ffn(pfx + 'experts' + e,
128	                                  self.cfg.moe_intermediate_size, is_expert=True)
129	        m.experts = experts.build()
130	
131	        shared = self.ffn(pfx + 'shared_experts',
132	                          self.cfg.intermediate_size * self.cfg.n_shared_experts)
133	
134	        return m.build(), shared
135	
136	    def layers(self, pfx):
137	        layers = ModuleListBuilder(ModuleListConfig(), self._ctx)
138	        for i, p in pfx.slices(0, self.cfg.num_hidden_layers):
139	            d = DecoderLayerBuilder(DecoderLayerConfig(), self._ctx)
140	            d.attention_norm = self.norm(p + 'input_layernorm')
141	            d.attention = self.attn(p + 'self_attn')
142	            d.ffn_norm = self.norm(p + 'post_attention_layernorm')
143	            if self.cfg.mlp_layer_types[i] == 'sparse':
144	                d.moe_ffn, d.feed_forward = self.moe(p + 'mlp')
145	            else:
146	                d.feed_forward = self.ffn(p + 'mlp', self.cfg.intermediate_size)
147	            layers[i] = d.build()
148	        return layers.build()
149
